title: Diagnose a deadlock from INNODB STATUS
description: A repeatable procedure that reads the LATEST DETECTED DEADLOCK block to find the lock-order conflict and fix it.
body: Use when the application logs intermittent ER_LOCK_DEADLOCK errors. Actions, in order: run SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS and read the LATEST DETECTED DEADLOCK section; identify the two transactions, the rows and indexes each holds and waits on, and the acquisition order; then make all code paths lock rows in a single consistent order, or add an index so the predicate locks fewer rows. Verify by replaying the two concurrent paths in a test and confirming the deadlock no longer fires. Validation signals include the deadlock section staying stale across a load run and the error counter flatlining. Risk boundary: deadlocks are expected and the loser is rolled back, so the app must retry the victim transaction; escalate only if the retry storm itself becomes the bottleneck.
